title1:
Fabio Massimo Capogrosso,
Everyone has his story
subtitle1:
Fabio Massimo Capogrosso, the first Composer-in-Residence of the Filarmonica “Arturo Toscanini” of Parma, is widely regarded as one of the most accomplished composers of his generation.



A composer of symphonic, chamber, theatrical, and film music, he has been featured by leading national and international institutions and festivals, including the Accademia Nazionale di Santa Cecilia, Teatro alla Scala in Milan, Teatro La Fenice in Venice, the IUC Concert Series, the Scuola Normale Superiore in Pisa, the San Francisco International Piano Festival, the Rebecca Penneys Piano Festival, Villa Pennisi in Musica, Teatro Grande in Brescia, the University of Michigan, and the Barness Music Recital Hall at the University of South Florida.



He is among the winners of the ninth edition of Discover America, the prestigious competition organized by the Chicago Ensemble, as well as the First Prize winner of the Keuris Composers Contest 2018.

He has collaborated with renowned director Marco Bellocchio, who selected him to compose the soundtracks for the films Exterior Night (2022) and Kidnapped (2023), both presented at the Cannes Film Festival, as well as for Bellocchio’s short film If I May (Chapter II), screened out of competition at the 81st Venice International Film Festival.

For the soundtrack of Exterior Night, he was nominated for Best Composer at the David di Donatello Awards 2023 and received the Apulia Soundtrack Award 2023 as Composer of the Year.

He composed the score for Gianluca Jodice’s film Le Déluge: The Last Days of Marie Antoinette (2023), earning nominations at both the Palmarès Européen de la Bande Originale and the Nastri d’Argento Awards. He also composed the music for Francesca Comencini’s The Time It Takes (2024), presented out of competition at the 81st Venice International Film Festival, for which he received the Special Prize at the Soundtrack Stars Awards, along with nominations at the Nastri d’Argento and the Palmarès Européen de la Bande Originale. For A Brief Love Story by Ludovica Rampoldi, he won the Gianni Lenoci Award for Best Original Score at Sudestival.

On the occasion of the 700th anniversary of Marco Polo’s death, he was commissioned by Teatro La Fenice in Venice to compose Confini, a work for choir and orchestra inspired by the great Venetian traveler. The piece premiered on April 19, 2024, performed by the Orchestra and Choir of Teatro La Fenice under the direction of Maestro Nicola Luisotti, as part of the 2023/2024 symphonic season.

He is also the subject of the documentary Beyond the Mask, directed by Andrea Campajola and produced by Edizioni Curci and CIDIM – Italian National Music Committee.

Fabio Massimo Capogrosso’s works are published by CAM Sugar, Edizioni Curci, and Plaza Mayor Company
